course.name: Claude Code for AI Operators
course.author: Tom Crawshaw
course.description: Learn to investigate, identify, and build real automations using Claude Code

modules.name: AI Operator Fundamentals
modules.description: Master Claude Code for automation and process improvement work

lessons.name: Introduction & The AI Operator Role
lessons.description: Welcome, scenario reveal, automation mindset, OpCode setup

lessons.name: Investigating Manual Processes
lessons.description: Navigate messy docs, extract opportunities, create automation map

lessons.name: Working with Data & APIs
lessons.description: Parse CSV/JSON, connect to Airtable, Google Sheets, transform data

lessons.name: Introduction to MCPs
lessons.description: Install MCPs, extend capabilities, optional Synta for n8n

lessons.name: Agents & Parallel Processing
lessons.description: Use agents for API research, parallel processing, faster reports

lessons.name: Custom Sub-Agents for Operations
lessons.description: Build Process Analyzer and API Expert agents, persistent helpers

lessons.name: CLAUDE.md: Your Automation Playbook
lessons.description: Document patterns, create standards, build institutional knowledge

lessons.name: Automation Design & Planning
lessons.description: From manual process to automation spec, requirements gathering

lessons.name: Project 1: PDF Invoice Processing
lessons.description: Local PDF folder → Airtable, extract data, error handling

lessons.name: Project 2: Google Drive Automation
lessons.description: Watch Drive folder, auto-process PDFs, scheduled automation

lessons.name: Project 3: Invoice Upload Web App
lessons.description: Build upload interface, process backend, deploy to Vercel
lessons.deliverable: Live web app URL anyone can use

lessons.name: Version Control with GitHub
lessons.description: GitHub basics, setup repo, commit code, create documentation

lessons.name: Documentation & Handoff
lessons.description: Create runbooks, train users, maintainable library

scenario.goal: Identify automation opportunities and build working solutions
